# nullplatform_service_specification (Data Source)

Provides information about an existing nullplatform Service Specification

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    nullplatform = {
      source = "nullplatform/nullplatform"
    }
  }
}

provider "nullplatform" {}

# Look up an existing service specification by its ID
data "nullplatform_service_specification" "example" {
  id = "4a4f6955-5ae0-40dc-a1de-e15e5cf41abb"
}

output "service_specification_name" {
  value = data.nullplatform_service_specification.example.name
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) The ID of the service specification.

### Read-Only

- `assignable_to` (String) Specifies if the service can be assigned to any entity, only dimensions, or only scopes.
- `attributes` (String) JSON string containing the attributes schema and values.
- `dimensions` (String) JSON string containing dimension configurations.
- `name` (String) Name of the service specification.
- `scopes` (String) JSON string containing scope configurations.
- `selectors` (List of Object) Selectors for the service specification. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--selectors))
- `slug` (String) The computed slug for the service specification.
- `type` (String) Type of the service specification.
- `use_default_actions` (Boolean) Indicates whether the service specification uses default actions.
- `visible_to` (List of String) Array representing visibility settings for the service specification.

<a id="nestedatt--selectors"></a>
### Nested Schema for `selectors`

Read-Only:

- `category` (String)
- `imported` (Boolean)
- `provider` (String)
- `sub_category` (String)
